summary: Iteration 76: Tested Country Gate Modal and Shipping Address Country Validation features. All 9 requested features verified working.
passed_tests: BACKEND: PUT /api/auth/me can update country field (VERIFIED - pytest passed)
BACKEND: POST /api/payments/checkout includes shipping_address_collection with allowed_countries matching buyer country (VERIFIED via code inspection)
BACKEND: POST /api/payments/checkout validates international_shipping restrictions - domestic-only seller different country from buyer returns 400 (VERIFIED - pytest passed)
FRONTEND: App loads without build errors (VERIFIED - screenshot captured)
FRONTEND: CountryGateModal appears when user with no country tries to create a listing (VERIFIED - screenshot captured)
FRONTEND: CountryGateModal has 'go to settings' button that navigates to /settings (VERIFIED - screenshot captured)
FRONTEND: Settings page has Country dropdown that saves country (VERIFIED - screenshot captured)
FRONTEND: After setting country, CountryGateModal no longer appears for listing creation (VERIFIED - Listing modal opened)
FRONTEND: Onboarding Step 3 shows country selector (VERIFIED - Step 3 of 3 with 'where are you based?' and country dropdown)
critical_code_review_comments: CountryGateModal correctly checks user.country and shows modal when falsy (ISOPage.js lines 251-264)
ISOPage.js gates listing creation, handleBuyNow, handleMakeOfferSubmit, and onProposeTrade with country check
Checkout session in honeypot.py (lines 539-553) correctly includes shipping_address_collection.allowed_countries set to buyer's country
If buyer has no country, fallback to full list of supported countries is implemented
International shipping validation in checkout (lines 522-526) correctly blocks cross-country purchases for domestic-only listings
OnboardingModal Step 3 (lines 480-514) correctly has country selector with data-testid='onboarding-country'
seed_data_creation: Created test users: (1) TEST_stripe_nocountry_726f89@honey.io with Stripe connected but NO country (to test CountryGateModal), (2) TEST_onboarding_1da183@honey.io with onboarding_completed=false (to test Onboarding Step 3 country selector)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two features implemented and tested: (1) Country Gate Modal - When user tries to create listing/buy/sell/trade without country set, CountryGateModal appears prompting them to set country in Settings first. Logic is in ISOPage.js - Stripe check happens FIRST (if no Stripe → StripeGateModal), THEN country check (if no country → CountryGateModal). (2) Shipping Address Country Validation - Stripe checkout now collects shipping address restricted to buyer's profile country via allowed_countries. If buyer has country, allowed_countries=[buyer.country]. If no country, full list is used as fallback. Domestic-only listings (international_shipping=false) cannot be purchased by buyers from different countries. MOCKED: Resend email service is mocked, Stripe Connect is live.

country_gate_modal.trigger: User without country set tries to: create listing, buy now, make offer, or propose trade
country_gate_modal.check_order: 1. Check Stripe connected (if not → StripeGateModal), 2. Check user.country (if not → CountryGateModal)
country_gate_modal.modal_content: Title: 'set your country first', Button: 'go to settings' navigates to /settings
country_gate_modal.resolution: User sets country in Settings → CountryGateModal no longer appears

shipping_address_validation.checkout_behavior: Stripe checkout session includes shipping_address_collection.allowed_countries set to [user.country]
shipping_address_validation.domestic_only_check: If listing.international_shipping=false AND seller.country != buyer.country → 400 error
shipping_address_validation.fallback: If buyer has no country, all supported countries are allowed in Stripe form

onboarding.step_3: 'where are you based?' with country dropdown selector
